description_long: This object is a vintage-style, free-standing printer or tape drive unit, characterized by a rectangular, upright cabinet with a dark gray body and a prominent red-orange front panel. The front features two large, circular reels or spools, likely for magnetic tape, set behind a transparent or open section, with mechanical arms and rollers visible beneath. Above the reels is a control panel with several colored buttons and indicator lights, housed in a white, boxy enclosure that slightly protrudes from the top. The sides and back of the unit are plain, solid, and unadorned, with a ribbed texture on the top surface for ventilation. The overall design suggests a robust, industrial-grade appliance, possibly used in archival, data processing, or office environments.
description: A vintage, upright printer or tape drive with a dark gray body, red-orange front panel, dual reels, and a white control panel on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red-orange panel, dual reels, mechanical components, and the white control panel with buttons.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain, solid dark gray surface with the control panel protruding at the top rear.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a featureless, flat dark gray back with the control panel's rear edge visible at the top.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a plain dark gray surface and the control panel protruding at the top rear.
attribute_control_panel_design: A rectangular white control panel is located on top of the unit, featuring a row of colored buttons and indicator lights arranged horizontally.
attribute_filament_spool_holder: Two integrated spool holders are present on the front, holding large circular reels above the red-orange panel.
